meta.framework_compatibility: AURA Framework - HP Instant Ink Automation
meta.resolution_notes: This test case (TC_REG_001) describes a GENERIC user registration flow that does NOT match the HP Instant Ink domain that AURA framework automates. AURA is specialized for HP Instant Ink subscription management (enrollment, plan selection, cancellation, admin operations). The test case requires a generic RegistrationPage with username/email/password/first name/last name/terms checkbox - none of which exist in the AURA framework. AURA has HPIDPage for HP ID authentication, but it serves a different purpose (HP account sign-in/sign-up for Instant Ink enrollment). RESOLUTION: Include foundation files that every test needs, but mark ALL test-specific registration components as TO CREATE since they don't exist in the HP Instant Ink domain.

test_cases.title: Verify User Registration with Valid Data
test_cases.resolution_summary: Foundation and infrastructure files are available in the AURA framework. However, this test case describes a GENERIC user registration flow (username, email, password, first name, last name, terms checkbox, success page) that does NOT exist in the AURA framework. AURA is specialized for HP Instant Ink subscription management and has HPIDPage for HP ID authentication (different purpose). ALL page objects, locators, and methods specific to this generic registration flow need to be created.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Provides test setup, email generation for unique test data, screenshot capture on failure, and configuration management.
relevant_files.import_statement: from test_flows_common.test_flows_common import setup, generate_email, take_screenshot, accept_all_cookies, get_config
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this is a mandatory foundation file for ALL tests. Provides setup(), generate_email(), take_screenshot(), and other utilities.

relevant_methods: Browser and page lifecycle management methods
relevant_files.reason: Infrastructure file required for every test. Manages browser initialization, page creation, and browser lifecycle.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this is mandatory infrastructure for browser management in all tests.

relevant_files.reason: Infrastructure file required for every test. Provides configuration settings, constants, and logger.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this is mandatory infrastructure for configuration and logging.

relevant_files.reason: Infrastructure file required for every test. Provides test validation reporting and Allure integration.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms AllureReporter is available as session-scoped fixture for validation reporting.

relevant_files.reason: Base class for all page objects. Required whenever any page object is used. Provides ElementLocators attribute-based access and WaitProxy utilities.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms: 'If ANY page object (pages/*.py) is in the resolution → pages/base_page_object.py MUST be included.' BasePageObject provides ElementLocators and WaitProxy.

relevant_files.reason: Core utilities file. Provides ElementsDict and common utility functions used across the framework.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms core/utils.py provides core utility functions including ElementsDict(dict) custom dictionary class.

relevant_methods: Fixtures: stage_callback, tc_tracer, main_execution, reporter, session_cleanup
relevant_files.reason: Pytest configuration and fixtures. Required for test execution, reporting, and cleanup.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms conftest.py provides pytest fixtures including reporter() for AllureReporter, session_cleanup(), and test result capture hooks.

relevant_files.reason: YAML test configuration loading. Provides fixtures for YAML-driven test execution.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms core/conftest.py provides YAML configuration loading with fixtures yaml_data(), testcase_id(), and methods load_yaml_file(), parse_flow().

relevant_locators_summary.note: NO LOCATORS from the AURA framework are relevant to this test case. The test requires a generic RegistrationPage with username/email/password/first name/last name/terms checkbox fields. AURA framework is specialized for HP Instant Ink and does not have these generic registration form locators. ALL locators needed for this test must be created.

test_cases.flow_pattern: GENERIC USER REGISTRATION FLOW (NOT HP INSTANT INK): Navigate to registration page → Fill username → Fill email → Fill password → Fill confirm password → Fill first name → Fill last name → Accept terms checkbox → Submit registration → Verify success message or dashboard. This flow does NOT match the HP Instant Ink enrollment domain that AURA automates.

components_to_create.name: RegistrationPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the generic user registration page. Must inherit from BasePageObject and contain all locators for registration form fields (username, email, password, confirm password, first name, last name, terms checkbox, register button, error messages) and action methods (enter_username, enter_email, enter_password, etc.).
components_to_create.reason: KB confirms AURA framework does NOT have a generic RegistrationPage. AURA has HPIDPage for HP ID authentication (HP Instant Ink domain), but this test requires a generic registration page with username/email/password/first name/last name/terms checkbox. This page object does not exist in the framework.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: KB query confirmed: AURA framework is specialized for HP Instant Ink subscription management. No generic RegistrationPage exists. HPIDPage exists but serves different purpose (HP ID sign-in/sign-up for Instant Ink enrollment).

components_to_create.kb_evidence: No registration form locator found in KB for AURA framework.

components_to_create.name: DashboardPage

components_to_create.kb_evidence: KB has DashboardSideMenuPage and OverviewPage for HP Instant Ink navigation, but no generic DashboardPage for post-registration verification.

components_to_create.kb_evidence: No generic registration page navigation method found in KB.

components_to_create.description: Composite method to fill all registration form fields (username, email, password, confirm_password, first_name, last_name). Should accept page object and data parameters.
components_to_create.reason: KB does not document a method to fill a generic registration form. HPIDHelper has sign_up() for HP ID, but this is different context.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: No generic registration form fill method found in KB.

components_to_create.kb_evidence: No generic registration page load verification method found in KB.

components_to_create.name: RegistrationHelper
components_to_create.description: Helper class to orchestrate the complete registration flow. Should provide static methods like complete_registration(page, username, email, password, first_name, last_name) that coordinates page object interactions.
components_to_create.reason: KB does not document a helper for generic user registration. AURA has EnrollmentHelper for HP Instant Ink enrollment, but this test requires a generic registration helper.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: No generic registration helper found in KB. EnrollmentHelper exists but is for HP Instant Ink enrollment, not generic registration.

test_cases.dependency_notes: This test case requires a GENERIC user registration flow that does NOT exist in the AURA framework. AURA is specialized for HP Instant Ink subscription management (enrollment, plan selection, cancellation, admin operations). The test requires RegistrationPage with username/email/password/first name/last name/terms checkbox - none of which exist in AURA's HP Instant Ink domain. Only foundation and infrastructure files are available. ALL test-specific components must be created.

anti_hallucination_verification.notes: Extensive KB verification performed. Confirmed AURA framework is specialized for HP Instant Ink domain and does NOT have generic registration components. All foundation files verified to exist. All test-specific registration components verified to NOT exist and marked as TO_CREATE.

critical_findings: Test case TC_REG_001 describes a GENERIC user registration flow that does NOT match the HP Instant Ink domain that AURA framework automates.
AURA framework is specialized for HP Instant Ink subscription management (enrollment, plan selection, dashboard, cancellation, admin operations).
The test requires RegistrationPage with username/email/password/first name/last name/terms checkbox - NONE of these exist in AURA's HP Instant Ink domain.
AURA has HPIDPage for HP ID authentication (HP account sign-in/sign-up for Instant Ink enrollment), but this serves a different purpose than generic registration.
Only foundation and infrastructure files (8 files) are relevant. ALL test-specific registration components (19 components) must be created.
This represents a domain mismatch: test case is for generic web app registration, framework is for HP Instant Ink subscription management.
recommendations: Verify that this test case is intended for the AURA framework. AURA is specialized for HP Instant Ink, not generic user registration.
If this test is correct, create a new registration module with RegistrationPage, SuccessPage, DashboardPage, and RegistrationHelper.
Consider whether this test should use AURA's existing HPIDPage/HPIDHelper for HP ID account creation instead of generic registration.
If generic registration is needed, implement all 19 components marked as TO_CREATE before attempting test script generation.
Ensure the application under test actually has a generic registration page separate from HP ID sign-up.
